summary: Credential and wallet harvester exfiltrating to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: Package name advertises a 'crypto credential scanner' and the shipped code matches that purpose against the installer rather than for the installer. wallet-scanner.js enumerates browser/wallet artifacts and POSTs results to a hardcoded remote endpoint; scanner.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), username via execSync('whoami')-shape calls, filesystem probes via fs.existsSync) and beacons to https://ddjidd564.github.io (a personal GitHub Pages site, an unusual destination for any legitimate scanner) using https.request POST and follow-up curl invocations. build-verifier.js similarly combines os/fs/https/child_process to gather hostname/username/cwd and POST it outbound, and additionally runs `docker push` from a build-time helper. The combination of (a) hardcoded non-publisher C2 on github.io, (b) host/user/filesystem enumeration shipped to that C2 over HTTPS, and (c) a wallet-targeting sibling module is the credential/wallet stealer fingerprint. Installing or loading this package puts host identity, filesystem probe results, and any discovered wallet/credential material into attacker hands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
